subject: Chemistry
title: Making Plastic from Oil
description: A 60-second lesson on Making Plastic from Oil.

hook.text: Polymerization turns tiny oil-derived molecules into long chains. Those chains are plastic.

buildup.text: In 1907, Leo Baekeland heated formaldehyde and phenol under pressure. He created Bakelite—the first true plastic.

discovery.text: Polymers form when small monomers link together in chains of thousands. Their properties depend on the chain shape.

twist.text: Plastic was hailed as a miracle material. Now 380 million tons are made annually—most ending up as pollution.

climax.text: The same reaction that gave us medical devices and phones also created an environmental crisis.

punchline.text: Small molecules chain together. Miracle and crisis in one reaction.

quiz.question: What is polymerization?
options: Small molecules linking into long chains to form plastics
Breaking plastic down into its original components
Mixing two liquids to form a solid
